entry.title: Re: Converting C Code to Abap
entry.content:
You have already asked people to give you ABAP code to solve Sudoku, you were given the algorithm, and now you are asking to convert C code to ABAP.

It should take a few hours to write ABAP from the algorithm (unless you are new to coding or to ABAP language).

What issue do you have with the algorithm or with the ABAP language?

entry.title: Plastic Tax Spain SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
On January 1st 2023, a new tax came into effect in Spain that levies the use of non-recycled and non-reusable plastic packaging in Spain, aiming to gradually eliminate the use of these packages. It is an indirect and environmental tax with broad business repercussions, as it affects companies:

  • Engaged in the manufacture or distribution of packaging.
  • Conducting imports or intra-community acquisitions of non-reusable plastic packaging, whether empty or containing any type of merchandise. This includes non-reusable plastics used for closing or presenting products.

 

This tax is regulated in Chapter I of Title VII of Law 7/2022, of April 8, on waste and contaminated soils for a circular economy (Articles 67 to 83). Here is the link to the Spanish Tax Agency

 

https://sede.agenciatributaria.gob.es/Sede/impuestos-especiales-medioambientales/impuesto-especial-sobre-envases-plastico-reutilizables/informacion-general.html

 

We would appreciate it if you could provide us with detailed information on how SAP Cloud Public addresses these specific legal requirements.

 

We look forward to your prompt response and thank you in advance for your attention to this request. If you require additional information from our side to address this inquiry more accurately, please do not hesitate to let us know.

entry.title: Re: Text Editor Using CL_GUI_TEXTEDIT Displaying Text from Internal Table
entry.content: @thomas_mller13 No need to ask, search and you'll get the answer at many places, even in the English Wikipedia.

entry.title: Unable to download in DMS using CAPM NodeJS
entry.content: I am able to upload file and download file from postman successfully in DMS .Also completed the upload part from external file and I am able to download it from postman. I am getting the buffer but while performing from NodeJS get call for download using ?objectId='<value>' for downloading from my attachment(also passed responseType:buffer in get call). Also passed content-type: '<file-mime-type>',content-disposition. Still facing some issue when I use external service for downloading. For example, if I upload a png file I am not able to see the image. Any help would be appreciated.

#deep #entity

entry.title: Re: How do you retrieve an PM equipment using a linked FI...
entry.content: The Asset number available in table ILOA field ANLNR. It can be fetched based on ILOAN(Location and account assignment). Get the ILOAN from EQUZ using Equipment number and valid to (EQUZ-DATBI) >= system date or = 31.12.9999. Hope it helps.

entry.title: Minimum Settings Required for Automatic Release of Purchase Contract
entry.content:
I would like to set the minimum required for "Automatic Release of Purchase Contract."
Question: Do I need to set all the steps below?
I have set only step1, step2, step3 and step8. Is there any problem?
---------------------------------------------------------------

step1.MANDATORY: Check Automatic Workflow Customizing
step2.MANDATORY : Activate Flexible Workflow Scenarios 
step3.MANDATORY : Define Step Names and Decision Options in My Inbox
step4.MANDATORY : Define Visualization Metadata for My Inbox
step5.MANDATORY : Deactivate Event Type Linkages for SAP Business Workflows
step6.MANDATORY : Generalize Tasks and Activate Event Type Linkages for Flexible Workflow
step7.MANDATORY : Register Gateway Service for Value Helps
step8.Optional: Adapt Flexible Workflow Settings
step9.Optional: Adapt the Conditions Under which the Flexible Workflow is Restarted

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Minimum Settings Required for Automatic Release of Pu...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

I have checked and the steps marked as mandatory are indeed needed to ensure consistency for the workflow. 

I would advise to ensure to carry out all mandatory steps as mentioned in SAP Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/be616f1ebef243dda20b5e862a4fbc38.html?locale=en-US&version=2022.latest

For example there are known issues if the task is not set as General. Issues where the is a delay in the workitem reaching the approver, and others. 

All steps are important, but to ensure consistency, please ensure to follow through on any marked as mandatory. 

Kind Regards, 
Lisa
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 1 - S/4HANA OData

entry.title: Re: Parsing and dividing barcode into material and batch...
entry.content:
Hello @angelbryan,

Order POD does not support barcode parsing out of the box yet, but you can always evaluate custom development/extension possibilities, see: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing?task=develop_task for more details.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: SAP Background Job Processing

Thank you @Mouni_Priyanka for putting these steps together. Pretty helpful and something that every SAP Consultant must be aware of!

entry.title: Re: Issue with EDI 856 to DELVRY07 IDOC mapping in CPI -...
entry.content:
Hi Das,

To achieve this, you need to use pre-process process direct and post processing process direct.

In the pre-process process direct, you need to use an XSLT script (it is bit tricky, and complex based on your requirement) to refer all the parent/child looping nodes sequence in HL* segment, and then after mapping (MAG), use post processing process direct to align/sort them as per the sequence provided in the HL* Segment.

I am not providing any sample code here, as it takes more time to build, try yourself and its definitely possible with the solution provided.

Thanks

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: PRT details not getting copied from master routing.
entry.content:
Hello Harsh,

As you can see here, PRT assignments in Master Routing are currently not copied over to order-specific routing during enrichment. You can evaluate using Automatic Triggers, Business Rules, to trigger a PPD on Order Creation and evaluate if you can assign PRTs.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Documentation: Running Approuter - update Version
entry.content: Please provide any updated doc for "@sap/approuter": "^16.0.0", Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Charts are not displaying in Webi Reports after patch...
entry.content:
I'm taking a guess...

Make sure you still have an Adaptive Processing Server that supports the Visualization Service.  I believe this service supports charts in Webi documents.

entry.title: Re: Local(DM) PRT material stock not getting updated on T...
entry.content:
Hello Harsh,

Logging Tool usage does not consume inventory ootb at all.
If you want to consume any materials, you must add it to BOM and consume accordingly.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: PRT details not getting copied from master routing.
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,

I went through the help doc. Indeed my scenario of directly copying from master routing to order-specific is not possible. I am looking at another option of getting PRT details from ERP itself along with the order but I'm facing an issue there as well. I have asked in detail on a comm post here. Please have a look at it.

Thanks and Regards,
Harsh.

entry.title: Re: Odata Query not working when using '=' symbol
entry.content:
You need to know, that query parameters (also called CGI parameters, based on an old standard of the 90s) have a special format, where the characters '?', '&', '+', '%' and '=' have a special meaning. Roughly speaking, the format of a URL with query parameters is:

http://hostname:port/path?name1=value1&name2=value2

The ? separates the query parameters from the rest of the URL, the & separates two query parameters from each other, and the = separates the query-name from the query-value. A % sign is used to indicate an "escape sequence" and a +-sign can be used to represent a space inside a name or value. For example a parameter "street" with value "Alan Turing Avenue 42" would be encoded as

?street=Alan+Turing+Avenue+42

That means, if you want to use any of these special characters (and some others) inside the name or value of a query parameter, you need to "URL-encode" it. See HTML URL Encoding Reference (w3schools.com)

In our example, we have a query parameter with the name "$filter" and the value "Account = '800000012341' and Prodtyp = 'EWR'". The = sign needs to be encoded as %3D, leading to the following correct query:

?$format=json&$filter=Account%20%3D%20'800000012341'%20and%20Prodtyp%20%3D%20'EWR'&$expand=NavtoData1%2DNavtoData2

(Note that the algorithm used here, encoded space characters as %20 instead of as a + sign, which is also ok. In general, every character can be encode ("escaped") by %xy, where xy is the hexadecimal value of its ASCII code or its UTF-8 code.)

entry.title: Re: MESSAGE TYPE X - The current application has intentionally triggered a termination with a short
entry.content: The text indicates you have a type issue because it says ZCL_ZDEEPENT_MPC_EXT=>TS_DEEP_ENTITY# does not exist. The character of note would be the # at the end of the name, which would have me looking at how the service is being called.  It would also be worth reviewing any static references for that type in the service implementation code.

entry.title: Re: PRT details not coming from ERP to DM
entry.content:
Hi,

In Integration Message Dashboard app, right after Order Download message, you should also see a Production Order PRT Download message. If you don't see that, check if your Collaboration Links are in place for PRT Download. See: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/execution/manage-collaboration-links

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Responsive vs. Canvas in SAP Analytics Cloud with Optimized Design Experience
entry.content:
Hello Dharani,

Your blog gives a apt insight into SAC Design Experience. The blog is well written.

entry.title: Creation of Outbound Delivery using VL01N in SAP
entry.content:
Delivery Replication SAP to Salesforce

1. Introduction

Outbound Delivery in SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing) refers to the process of fulfilling customer orders by shipping goods or products from a company's warehouse or production facility to the customer's specified location. This documentation provides a comprehensive guide to understanding and executing Outbound Delivery processes within SAP.

2. Purpose

The purpose of Outbound Delivery in SAP is to streamline the logistics and shipping operations of a company, ensuring accurate and timely delivery of products to customers. By efficiently managing outbound deliveries, businesses can enhance customer satisfaction, optimize inventory management, and improve overall operational efficiency.

3. Key Features

  • Creation of Outbound Delivery Orders (ODOs) based on sales orders or stock transport orders.
  • Packing of goods into delivery packages or handling units.
  • Generation of delivery documents such as delivery notes and shipping labels.
  • Integration with transportation management systems for route optimization and carrier selection.
  • Confirmation of goods issue to update inventory levels.
  • Monitoring and tracking of delivery status in real-time.

 

4. Process Flow

The typical process flow for Outbound Delivery in SAP involves the following steps:

  1. Sales Order Creation: Customer orders are received and entered into the SAP system as sales orders.
  2. Outbound Delivery Order Creation: Based on the sales orders, Outbound Delivery Orders (ODOs) are created in SAP. These ODOs contain information such as the items to be shipped, quantities, delivery dates, and customer shipping details.
  3. Packing: Goods are picked from the warehouse shelves or production lines and packed into delivery packages or handling units. The packing process involves assigning materials to specific delivery units and determining packaging materials.
  4. Goods Issue: Once the goods are packed and ready for shipment, a goods issue is posted in SAP to signify the removal of goods from inventory. This updates inventory levels and triggers the billing process.
  5. Delivery Document Printing: Delivery documents such as delivery notes and shipping labels are generated in SAP and printed for inclusion with the shipment.
  6. Shipment Execution: The goods are physically transported to the customer's location using selected carriers or transportation methods.
  7. Delivery Confirmation: Upon successful delivery, the delivery is confirmed in SAP to update the delivery status and trigger subsequent processes such as billing and customer invoicing.

T.code:-

  • VL01N – With / Without Reference to Sales Order.
  • VL02N – Single Document Change.
  • Vl03N – Display Delivery Document.

 

  • In the initial Screen of Create Outbound Delivery Need to provide below information.
  • Shipping Point: Get the shipping point from the shipping tab of sales order line item.
  • Selection Date : It should be either the material availability date or the system copies the item into the delivery.
  • Sales Order Number: The sales order number against which the delivery document needs to be created.
  • From and to Item Number: Line item number of sales order against which needs to be created delivery, if a user does not specify any line item then the system will consider all line items of sales order for delivery creation.

 

Create outbound delivery without order reference

 

Then the system will land to the user on Create Delivery Overview screen like the below screenshot.

 

 

 

 

Picking Tab display

  • Plant code: Delivery plant
  • Storage location
  • Delivery Quantity
  • UoM
  • Picked Quantity

The picked quantity is grey out mode that means we have to transfer the order to pick the material.

 

 

Transport tab provide information

  • Transportation Planning date
  • Transportation Planning status.
  • Route
  • Gross weight
  • Volume

 

 

 

 

The goods movement tab provides information that which movement type is used for this delivery document.

 

Select any one line item of the delivery document and click on the item Details button. It will display item data of that line item.

 

 

The predecessor data tab provides information about

  • Reference Document number e.g. Sales order number.
  • Reference Item: line item number of Sales order number.
  • Reference Type: Document category.

 

Click on SAVE button.

 

Now, the delivery document has been saved with the delivery number.

5. Benefits

  • Improved order fulfilment accuracy and speed.
  • Enhanced visibility and traceability of outbound shipments.
  • Reduction in shipping errors and delays.
  • Optimal utilization of warehouse resources.
  • Better customer service and satisfaction.

6. Conclusion

Outbound Delivery in SAP plays a critical role in ensuring the smooth and efficient execution of logistics and shipping operations. By leveraging SAP's robust functionality and integration capabilities, businesses can effectively manage outbound deliveries, meet customer expectations, and drive operational excellence.

entry.title: Re: Issue with EDI 856 to DELVRY07 IDOC mapping in CPI - IA - MAG
entry.content: The information I provided on the previous query is still relevant. You would need to split into multiple STs because the cardinality is 1 ST -> 1 IDoc.

entry.title: Value Help RAP V4 SAP__common.ValueListReferences path ../srvd_f4/sap/..
entry.content:
Dear Community,

In the scenario of creating an SAP Fiori Elements application with RAP and OData V4 on S/4HANA Public Cloud, I'm using a standard CDS for value help consumption (e.g.  I_ProductStdVH), using the well kown and documented @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition annotation.

If I try to preview the entity I'can't see any data from the search help:

exploring the $metadata, the endpoint redirects to a generic path "srvd_f4", and it refers to the annotation 'SAP__common.ValueListReferences' in the property of the field.

if I try to open the relative endopoint with /srvd_f4/...

I see the error Service 'I_PRODUCTSTDVH' repository 'SRVD_F4' is not assigned to group 'Z_XXXX'.

I'm in S/4HANA Public Cloud and I don't have access to /iwbep/v4_admin

In addition, I see this opened issue https://github.com/SAP/odata-library/issues/131  that seems related 

do you have any suggestions?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: PRT details not coming from ERP to DM
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,

I checked the configuration and collaboration links were missing. After adding the directives, prod order prt download is also happening and the PRTs are visible with orders. 

Thanks and Regards,
Harsh.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content:
afaik, Crystal doesn't support https.  But you can do this using a Crystal UFL (User Function Library).
At least one of the 3rd-party UFLs listed here provides a function that supports getting an image from an https url.

Better yet -- one of these UFLs also provides functions to directly generate various types of barcode images (including QR) and dynamically load them into the report (using the Graphic Location expression of a dummy image).

Ganesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Test System
entry.content:
Hello,

I think the advise given from Margit is valuable. 

Normaly SAP System have three stages, D(evelopment), QA, P(roduction). Sometimes you have more.

So I think you should populate your system with data (I wonder why there is none), because you want to learn how SAP in your organisations works.

For development and testing you need a customized system like your production. Can you no get access to your development or QA system?

My two cent

entry.title: can I explode BOM in repair order process in customer services
entry.content:
for the in-house repair process firstly, I have created a return order with the follow-up activity in-house repair. in the in-house repair order, I have a query related below issue.

In the in-house repair order process, when we create a repair order for a material, is it possible to run MRP for that material?

when we create a return order for the in-house repair process, is it possible to apply 8 defective processes for that return material?

Can we explode BOM in repair order to know which material is needed to repair the product?

If possible, kindly let me know the process.

Thanks & Regards,

Rajeswari.B

entry.title: Re: Converting C Code to Abap

entry.title: Custom Vendor Payment Method in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
How to add Custom Vendor(Supplier)/Customer Payment Method in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom Vendor Payment Method in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
You can use SSCUI 101972 "Set Up Payment Methods for Each Country/Region for Payment Transactions" 

In the posting details you can specify whether it is a vendor payment or customer payment by choosing the "Document Type for Payment".

You can refer the help documentation on Configuring Payment Methods

that annotations are not supported

entry.title: Transforming Business Operations with an AI Copilot
entry.content:
AI is rapidly changing the landscape of business operations across various industries. One of the key applications of AI in businesses today are AI copilots. These AI-powered tools are designed to understand, learn, and respond to human language in a contextual manner. They offer numerous benefits, including operational efficiency, automating manual tasks, improved customer engagement, and data-driven decision-making.

AI copilots work by utilizing advanced algorithms and machine learning techniques to understand and respond to user queries and requests. They are designed to process and analyze vast amounts of data in real-time, enabling them to generate accurate and relevant responses. They rely on natural language processing to comprehend the nuances of human language and extract meaning from text or speech input. AI copilots continuously learn and improve their performance. They are trained on massive datasets, which let them recognize patterns, make predictions, and provide personalized recommendations.

SAP Business AI is already embedded in the SAP applications that customers already use to run their most critical business processes. With SAP Business AI, you are empowered to interact with SAP software through Joule – SAP’s copilot available across SAP applications.

Joule lets you become more efficient and productive through simply describing your ideas, asking analytical questions, or telling the system what to do. With Joule, you can:

  • Streamline tasks across SAP applications
  • Get quick answers and smart insights on-demand
  • Get tailored content to jumpstart your work
  • Maintain full control over decision-making and your data privacy

The adoption of AI copilots in business operations is no longer a novelty but a necessity for staying competitive in the digital age. From improving customer service to enhancing operational efficiency and enabling data-driven decisions, AI assistants offer a myriad of benefits. As AI technology continues to evolve, businesses can expect even more sophisticated capabilities from AI copilots, reshaping the way businesses operate and interact with their customers and employees.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Learn more about SAP Business AI
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI copilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: Posting journal entry API causes "error "SRT: Plain SOAP: Reliable messaging (RM) configured..."
entry.content:
Hi, Experts,
I would like to post journal entry into S4 HANA cloud using "Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)"API ". But when I post it by Postman, I get the error "SRT: Plain SOAP: Reliable messaging (RM) configured, but no Message ID and no WSRM assertion provided." in S4 HANA cloud.  Is there anyone who can find the cause?

API Reference | Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud , API , SAP S/4HANA Finance 

Below XML body was posted :

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>

<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">

    <soap:Header>

        <wsse:Security soap:mustUnderstand="1" xmlns:wsse="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/2004/01/oasis-200401-wss-wssecurity-secext-1.0.xsd"/>

    </soap:Header>

    <soap:Body>

        <JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest xmlns="http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN">

            <MessageHeader>

                <ID schemeID="MSG_2024-03-28"/>

                <CreationDateTime>2024-03-28T12:00:00.1234567Z</CreationDateTime>

            </MessageHeader>

            <JournalEntryCreateRequest>

                <MessageHeader>

                    <ID/>

                    <CreationDateTime/>

                </MessageHeader>

                <JournalEntry>

                    <OriginalReferenceDocumentType>BKPFF</OriginalReferenceDocumentType>

                    <BusinessTransactionType>RFBU</BusinessTransactionType>

                    <AccountingDocumentType>KR</AccountingDocumentType>

                    <DocumentReferenceID>Test</DocumentReferenceID>

                    <OriginalReferenceDocument>XXXX</OriginalReferenceDocument>

                    <DocumentHeaderText>Test</DocumentHeaderText>

                    <CompanyCode>1510</CompanyCode>

                    <CreatedByUser>API User</CreatedByUser>

                    <DocumentDate>2024-03-28</DocumentDate>

                    <PostingDate>2024-03-28</PostingDate>

                    <Item>

                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>

                        <CompanyCode>1510</CompanyCode>

                        <GLAccount listID="0065001000"/>

                        <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="JPY">100</AmountInTransactionCurrency>

                        <DebitCreditCode>S</DebitCreditCode>

                        <DocumentItemText>text Test</DocumentItemText>

                        <Tax>

                            <TaxCode>V3</TaxCode>

                            <AccountAssignment>

                                <CostCenter>15101201</CostCenter>

                                <FunctionalArea>YB20</FunctionalArea>

                            </AccountAssignment>

                        </Item>

                        <CreditorItem>

                            <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>

                            <Creditor>15300001</Creditor>

                            <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="JPY">-100

                        </AmountInTransactionCurrency>

                            <DebitCreditCode>H</DebitCreditCode>

                        </CreditorItem>

                    </JournalEntry>

                </JournalEntryCreateRequest>

            </JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>

    </soap:Body>

</soap:Envelope>

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - build service not working since 24 hours
entry.content:
Hi all, thanks for your patience. There was an issue affecting some builds over the weekend, but it should be resolved and working now. If you experience issues again, please make a new post with required details!

cc @nicholas1959 @ahannula @Coutinho

For DME ZNL_GI_XML_CT the XML File generated, we can not see information regarding node Prtry, can you please help me?

Thanks

Best regards,

Alfredo

entry.title: S4 Public Cloud Development : "Logon to the ABAP service instance has failed" Error in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an issue while connecting to the cloud system (S4C not BTP) from eclipse. Post giving the URL, Eclipse asks to login via browser and when that is done, browser will authenticate successfully, but Eclipse shows error "Logon to the ABAP service instance has failed". I tried with copy URl option as well. Even that doesnt work. Any pointers would be helpful.

Thanks

Sreekanth

entry.title: Live Data Connectivity to Google Big Query
entry.content:
Good news for customers looking for live data access to Google BigQuery (GBQ)! With the release in Q4 of 2024, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) plans to get its first live SQL data access. Google BigQuery (GBQ) will be the first SQL data source consumed by SAC, as part of SAP’s partnership with Google.

Traditionally, live data access in SAC requires that the remote data source is a data warehouse, completed by semantic modelling and an OLAP engine to access the complete data model. With live SQL data access, SAC will be able to directly access fact data from customers' GBQ data lake, giving customers using GBQ the flexibility to decide whether they want to use acquired or live data within their models. With that, customers will no longer need to maintain load jobs from their data lakes.

Users will be able to choose between using a GBQ service user to access their data lake or individual authentication if user-based security is being used on the data lake. To maximize performance, SAC will use SQL pushdown wherever the source database can handle it.”

entry.title: Re: TU for delivery assignment / not found
entry.content: Have done it But not getting the idea

entry.title: Re: TU for delivery assignment / not found
entry.content: Have done it But its not getting updated in transportation planning type

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content: I'm new into this, i have no idea how to use these UFL's 😞 Is there no way that Crystal reports excepts https? Isn't anyway better than unsecured http?

entry.title: Re: TU for delivery assignment / not found
entry.content: I dont want the TM. Have deleted the assignment in " Allow EWM Execution Without Transportation Planning (Outbound)" configuration

entry.title: Re: CVI - Re-Run of Synchronization after post conversion
entry.content: Hi Drahul, we had the same problem during our upgrade and we had to resynchronize back from BP to vendor.  This was necessary to populate the bank ID field in the vendor data.  Normally this would happen automatically the next time a user updates the BP, but we were having an issue because the user could not select the bank ID in FB60.  There are more details in oss note 2781874.  I contacted SAP support to ask for a utility to fix the problem but they told me I had to resynchronize after upgrading.  We are only using bank data with some of our vendors so we identified those and resynchronized them rather than doing all BPs.

entry.title: Re: TU for delivery assignment / not found
entry.content: I dont want the TM. Have deleted the assignment in " Allow EWM Execution Without Transportation Planning (Outbound)" configuration even though its not updating

entry.title: Re: Customer / Supplier Business Partner Master Data: Authorization Groups
entry.content:
Hi @Stephen_Ward, 

I am in a case where we have two companies in the system, and the users that manage one company should not be able to see or modify the business partners of the other company, not even the basic address data. For this, it has been proposed to create two groupings of business partners, and to be able to restrict the visibility according to the grouping; and the problem is that we have not seen that this restriction can be used. At the basic data level of the business partner, there are only the authorization groups, but it does not work, since it is not possible to restrict according to the company.

Can someone help me to solve it? 

#bp #businesspartnerrestriction #saps4hanapubliccloud

Thanks a lot,

Regards

entry.title: SuccessFactors Learning Subject Matter Expert Office Hours (English)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Registration details can be found here (Authentication Required)

Please join us and our Recognized Expert partners as we jointly host our Subject Matter Expert office hours!

If you have questions about your SuccessFactors Learning product regarding best practices, use cases, 'how to' questions, etc. then please join our call and we'll have our expert answer your question live.

These sessions are held in an open online forum with other customers participating. There is no pre-planned content or presentation. Time is spent answering the questions you bring to the call. No advance registration required.

Note: Attendees can use the in-system audio and have the system call you directly or attendees can call in manually. To locate the call-in number for your region along with the passcode, please click on the information dropdown in the upper right corner of the meeting (indicated by an italicized i in a circle icon).

Each session is held twice per month.

PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Registration details can be found here (Authentication Required)

Please join us and our Recognized Expert partners as we jointly host our Subject Matter Expert office hours!

If you have questions about your SuccessFactors Recruiting product regarding best practices, use cases, 'how to' questions, etc. then please join our call and we'll have our expert answer your question live.

These sessions are held in an open online forum with other customers participating. There is no pre-planned content or presentation. Time is spent answering the questions you bring to the call. No advance registration required.

Note: Attendees can use the in-system audio and have the system call you directly or attendees can call in manually. To locate the call-in number for your region along with the passcode, please click on the information dropdown in the upper right corner of the meeting (indicated by an italicized i in a circle icon).

Each session is held twice per month.

entry.title: Generate Custom Legal Certificates in SAP DRC
entry.content:
The purpose of withholding tax is to ensure that the government receives tax revenue in a timely manner and to facilitate tax compliance. It is commonly applied to various types of income such as wages, salaries, interest, dividends, royalties, and payments to contractors or freelancers. The rates of withholding tax and the types of income subject to withholding tax vary depending on the tax laws of each jurisdiction.

While SAP provides standard reports for reporting withholding tax, custom requirements may arise for specific needs. This blog focuses on the development of a custom certificate to address such requirements. Developing a custom certificate requires knowledge in DRC extensibility and Adobe Form development.

In this scenario, a custom Adobe form is being created within the system, extending the Thailand 50 BIS certificate.

Go to t-code SFP to create the Adobe Form. The form can be developed using the default interface SOMU_DEFAULT_INTERFACE, with XFA2 chosen as the layout technology.

Once the form layout is created, save and activate it.
Then, navigate to Define Compliance Application and initiate the creation of a new report definition. For this example, an existing report definition is duplicated. Provide a name for the new report definition and specify a package for its storage.

Create custom report definition

 

Save it in local package

After creating a new report definition, Add a new document according to the specified requirements. In this instance, a PDF document is presented as an example.

Add new legal document

 

Create it as a PDF

Provide details for the document such as the document name, file name, code page, etc.

Document Details

Select a schema for this document, which defines the format and structure of the document. Various options for creating a schema are available, including using XSD, JSON, or ABAP dictionary. For this particular example, the schema is generated using the ABAP dictionary. Alternatively, an existing schema can also be utilized.

Create Schema

 

Enter the Custom Form Details

After finalizing the schema, proceed to the Mapping section. The structure of the document, based on the schema, will be displayed, allowing for mapping according to the requirements. Data can be mapped from various sources such as CDS, tables, class methods, or system fields.

Perform Mapping

Save and Activate the report definition.

Save and Activate

Now the schema needs to be downloaded in order to perform mapping in the Adobe form. Select the "Export Schema" option and then choose "Export as Simplified XSD". Ensure that the report definition is in editable mode before attempting to download the schema.

Download XSD as shown

The layout for the Adobe form can be created using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer (ALD). Develop a custom layout, and if there's a requirement for a fixed company logo, watermark, or signature, it can be directly incorporated. For dynamic company logo, watermark, or signature needs, include an image field in the Adobe Form. The value of this field can be manipulated during form generation

Create Custom Layout

In this example, an already developed Thailand 50 BIS form has been downloaded. It contains existing bindings. To remove these bindings, navigate to the data view, right-click, and select "Delete Data Connection". During deletion, you have the option to retain or delete the existing bindings. In this instance, the existing bindings are kept intact while mapping new fields in the form.

Delete Data Connection

 

Do not remove existing binding

To establish a new data connection, right-click in the Data View and select "New Data Connection". Upload the previously downloaded schema from the steps above and utilize it for mapping the new fields.

Create New Data Connection

 

Choose XML schema

 

Upload the earlier downloaded schema

Next, proceed with the new mapping process as demonstrated below. The newly added fields from the schema will be displayed, allowing them to be mapped within the form layout.

Perform Mapping

After completing the layout, upload it in transaction code SFP. Change the existing custom form and select "Upload Layout". This action will replace the existing layout with the new one.
Save and Activate the Form.

Now create a new report category , for this example exisiting report category is copied . Enter the Report category name and save it designated package .

Create New Report category

Maintain the report activity details by assigning the already developed report definition to this report category. Enter additional information such as description, phase, submission mode, valid form, and validity period.

Add new activity

Save and Activate the report category

Activity details

 

The Custom legal certificate generation involves several steps related to working with Adobe Forms and Report Definitions:

  1. Develop Custom Adobe Form: Design and create a personalized Adobe form tailored to specific needs or requirements.

  2. Create a New Report Definition: Create a new definition for a report, outlining its structure, content, and parameters.

  3. Create Schema for the Document: Develop a schema outlining the structure and format of the document to ensure consistency and compatibility.

  4. Download the Schema: Retrieve the schema for integration purposes.

  5. Perform Mapping In Form: Map data fields or elements within the Adobe form to corresponding elements in the schema or report definition.

  6. Create a New Report Category: Create a new category to classify or organize reports based on specific criteria or characteristics.

  7. Assign Report Definition to the Report Category: Link  the newly created report definition with the designated report category for generation of custom output.

Overall, these steps involve the creation, customization, and organization of Adobe forms and report definitions to effectively manage data and generate custom report within DRC framework.

Summary:

Summary

Thank you for reading!

Drop a like or comment below if you found the above steps useful and do let me know in the comments section if you are looking for any other specific use case of US report extensibility.

entry.title: Unable to run a query with secondary database in capm
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created a CAPM project which contains a primary database. Now i want to add secondary database to the same project and run raw queries specific to secondary database. 

 1. Added secondary db in package.json

2. created a custom event handler to fetch the records


this.on("getRecords",async(req) => {
  try {
    //Fetch data from primary database
    const primary_db_response = await cds.db.run(`<---Query1-->`); //Works fine
  
    //connect to secondary database
    const secondary_db = await cds.connect.to('secondary-database'); //Works fine
    let secondary_db_response = []

    //Create a transaction for secondary database
    await secondary_db.tx(async() => {
      secondary_db_response = await tx.run(`<---Query2-->`) //Fails as it runs under the primary database connection
    })
  } catch (error) {
    //Handle on error
  }
})

When trying to run a Query2 (SELECT Query) with secondary db transaction (Line 14), it results in error as tx.run() is still running under the primary database connection.

 

tx.run(`<---Query2-->`) results below error

Error: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found


Note: Query2 statement only contains the database table which are part of secondary database.

How to resolve above problem and make sure tx.run() runs under the secondary database connection?

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content:
A UFL, once installed, simply gives you additional functions within Crystal.

You point about how reasonable it is to support https is valid. But we don't control how Crystal behaves. We can, however, work around Crystal's limitations.

entry.title: I need to add a new field in CO11n
entry.content:
Hi all,

I need to add a new field as 'Customer' in production order confirmation screen(CO11n). Please let me know the process.

entry.title: PR is marked for deletion by APOUSER
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we know PR is sent from APO to ECC with background APOUSER.

I am having issue where PR is marked for deletion by APOUSER. How APO can do this ?

Similarly APOUSER is changing the Account Number of Supplier -

Please suggest how to check this and how we can stop this change in ECC.

entry.title: RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package
entry.content:
In today's competitive landscape, asset owners and service providers face the ongoing challenge of maximizing asset value and delivering exceptional services while minimizing downtime and costs. Embracing advanced asset and service management practices, such as outcome-based operations and services, is essential.

By prioritizing specific outcomes over traditional metrics, such as maximizing asset uptime or service response times, businesses can align their strategies with client needs, ensuring greater efficiency, reliability, and customer satisfaction. This shift towards outcome-based approaches allows for the optimization of asset performance and service delivery, ultimately driving greater value for both businesses and their clients.

Traditional asset and service management approaches often fall short in meeting these demands. Manual processes, disparate systems, and reactive maintenance practices can lead to inefficiencies, increased downtime, and higher operational costs. Moreover, with the complexity of modern assets and the rise of IoT (Internet of Things) devices, there's a pressing need for more intelligent, data-driven solutions to effectively manage assets and deliver superior services.

This is where the cutting-edge RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package comes into play, revolutionizing how businesses manage their assets and deliver top-notch services.

Introducing the Comprehensive Solution

The newly released RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package offering is a game-changer for asset owners and service providers alike. This integrated solution harnesses the power of advanced technologies such as AI (Artificial Intelligence), IoT, predictive analytics, and cloud computing to transform how assets are managed and services are delivered.

It allows companies to identify, plan, schedule, execute, close, and analyze maintenance and service work via closed-loop processes optimizing asset maintenance, ensuring minimal downtime, and maximizing operational efficiency.

Asset Health and Performance Optimization

At the core of this package is the ability to optimize maintenance strategies and measure and increase overall asset performance with out-of-the-box, self-service AI capabilities. By leveraging IoT sensors and AI algorithms, businesses can proactively detect potential issues before they escalate into costly failures. Predictive analytics help identify patterns and trends in asset behavior, enabling organizations to schedule maintenance activities more efficiently, optimize asset lifecycles, and minimize unplanned downtime.

Delivering Field Service Excellence

In addition to optimizing asset performance and reducing maintenance costs, the package empowers service providers to deliver exceptional service. In striving for field service excellence, digitalized field service management emerges as a cornerstone, offering a multitude of benefits including optimized operations and routes, efficient crew management, cost reduction, heightened first-time fix rates, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Leveraging AI-based schedule optimization further elevates performance by automating staff planning, simplifying scheduling processes, and maximizing resource utilization. Additionally, meticulous skills and qualifications management ensures assignments are delegated to engineers best suited for the task, promoting worker safety and delivering the highest level of professional service. Together, these strategies empower organizations to deliver exceptional field service experiences, driving efficiency, reliability, and customer satisfaction to new heights.

Mobile Accessibility

The package does not forget the workers on site. With role-based and persona-centric features, the intuitive mobile app empowers personnel for optimal task execution. Mobile-enabled processes revolutionize operations, prioritizing intelligence and automation across tasks such as work order management, inventory tracking, inspections, and safety permits. Furthermore, by integrating 3D visualizations, augmented reality (AR), and geo-spatial technology, maintenance and service activities are accelerated, providing on-device visual instructions and maps. Dynamic forms further enhance efficiency by seamlessly digitizing paper-based activities through an intuitive mobile forms designer. By embracing these innovative technologies, the package provides streamlined operations, boosted productivity, and fostered seamless communication between teams.

Workload Balancing and Capacity Levelling

In this integrated approach to asset management, the focus is on automating maintenance processes to enhance asset availability and efficiency. By leveraging asset data, rules, and policies, businesses can automate the generation of maintenance plans, ensuring optimal asset performance. Maintenance planning involves meticulous coordination of priorities, resources, and materials for various maintenance types, including planned, reactive, project-based, and inspection-based. Through AI-driven maintenance scheduling, tasks and resources are optimized, minimizing downtime, and maximizing utilization. During maintenance execution, comprehensive tracking and monitoring mechanisms provide full visibility into activities, facilitating continuous improvement efforts. This holistic approach streamlines maintenance operations, ultimately leading to enhanced asset management practices and improved overall performance.

Asset Collaboration

Finally, Network collaboration is key to optimizing asset management, and the RISE with SAP advanced asset and service management package brings the entire ecosystem onto a unified collaboration platform. With collaborative asset data management, the seamless flow of information across OEMs, component suppliers, operators, and service providers are facilitated, ensuring a comprehensive digital thread. Additionally, the platform enables collaborative maintenance and service operations by providing a shared space for work order collaboration between asset operators and service providers. By fostering collaboration across the asset management ecosystem, the solution enhances efficiency, transparency, and communication, ultimately driving improved asset performance and service delivery.

Key Features and Benefits

  • Closed loop processes: Connect every process from maintenance and service strategy, planning, and execution. 
  • Integrated Platform: A unified platform for asset management, maintenance, and service delivery, eliminating silos and improving collaboration across departments.
  • AI-Powered Insights: Advanced AI algorithms analyze vast amounts of data to uncover actionable insights, enabling data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement.
  • Predictive Analytics: Anticipate maintenance needs and asset failures before they occur, enabling proactive interventions and minimizing disruptions to operations.
  • Mobile Accessibility: Access critical asset and service information anytime, anywhere, through mobile applications, empowering field technicians and service teams to work more efficiently.
  • Scalability and Flexibility: Modular architecture allows for scalability and customization to meet the unique needs of different industries and organizations of all sizes.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the comprehensive package offering for advanced asset and service management represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage their assets and deliver services. By harnessing the power of advanced technologies, organizations can maximize asset performance, reduce maintenance costs, and deliver service excellence like never before. Whether you're an asset owner looking to optimize asset utilization or a service provider striving to exceed customer expectations, investing in an integrated Asset and Service Management solution is the key to staying ahead in today's competitive marketplace. Embrace the future of asset management and service delivery—transform your operations, drive efficiency, and unlock new opportunities for growth and success. Get started today with the RISE with SAP advanced service and asset management package.

 To learn more about Asset Health and Performance Optimization go to: SAP Asset Performance Management

To learn more about Delivering Field Service Excellence go to: SAP Field Service Management

To learn more about Mobile Accessibility go to: SAP Service and Asset Manager

To learn more about Workload Balancing and Capacity Levelling go to: SAP S/4HANA for Asset Management for resource scheduling

To learn more about Asset Collaboration go to SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content: Thanks for your replies! 🙂 But where do i install it? On the PC which generates the Report? Or just on my PC where i edit the Report?

entry.title: Re: MESSAGE TYPE X - The current application has intentionally triggered a termination with a short
entry.content: Ok, then that means you need to check the service implementation because the type that is inferred in the request handling doesn't exist at all.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 New Installation
entry.content:
Hello gonzalogomez, 

You can find the upgrade path in the overview note of your target release. for example, if you intend to upgrade to 10.0 SP2402, you can identify Upgrade Path Information in the SAP note #3413880 - Overview Note for SAP Business One 10.0 SP 2402.

Regarding your 2nd question about silient SQL server installation, it was possible long time ago when SQL server installer was very simple. now you need to install it manually in compliance with the required settings in SAP Business One Administrator's Guide.

 

Regards

EunSeok

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content: You install the UFL (it's just a dll) on any machine that needs to run the report.

entry.title: SAP CPIDS / CIDS: view design time data doesn't show a record, even if it is successful in history.
entry.content:
I am currently developing a new interface where IBP is the source and Target is the file. During the test it is successful, and I can see on the monitoring log that it was successfully picking up some row counts.



However when i try to use view design time data I wasnt able to pick up any records.
You may refer on the screen shot below for details on how i perform my desig time data.

 



Am I missing something?

entry.title: Re: How to work with CDS Views that are not released?
entry.content: Hi Jocelyn,

entry.title: Re: Dynamic QR-Code thru URL in a OLE-Object for printing...
entry.content: Which one is the UFL for creating a QR-Code from a "Number"?

entry.title: Re: Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytic
entry.content:
Great questions. The focus of this article is more on the capabilities of the product than the business aspects. That said, here are some thoughts I have on your questions: 

About 'Optimized Pricing': Optimizing price planning for maximum revenue involves analyzing various factors such as demand elasticity and competitor pricing. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides advanced tools, including predictive modeling and analytics, to help identify optimal price points based on historical data. While SAC can provide insights, achieving maximum revenue requires an understanding of market dynamics and consumer behavior. I believe that with SAC (and BTP) you have all the ingredients to prepare the menu.

Regarding 'reducing manual effort': SAC is designed to streamline processes, and that includes data feeds. Data or prices can come from SAP sources or non-SAP sources and with the capabilities of SAC you can put these numbers into your version and model. It depends a lot on how you tailor your planning. In my example, I copied from actual and plan into my Predictive_ML version (scenario). I can imagine connecting ECC sources as well. I also see great value in using DSP for all data management issues. With DSP and SAC working together, I can see even more sophisticated setups (like federated data) coming.

Thank you for your interest! Let's continue the conversation.

entry.title: DOCUMENT NUMBER GENERATION USING BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST.
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST is a standard SAP interface that allows you to post financial documents directly from an external system (like a non-SAP system) into SAP. This BAPI (Business Application Programming Interface) is primarily used for integrating external systems with SAP's Financial Accounting (FI) module.

Here are some common purposes for using BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST:

  1. Integration: It enables seamless integration between SAP and non-SAP systems for financial document posting. This is particularly useful when companies have legacy systems or third-party applications that need to interact with SAP FI.

  2. Automation: The BAPI allows for automating the posting of financial documents, reducing manual effort and potential errors. This is useful for high-volume transaction environments where manual entry is impractical.

  3. Data Consistency: By using BAPIs, companies can ensure data consistency between SAP and other systems. This helps in maintaining accurate financial records across the organization.

  4. Real-time Processing: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST facilitates real-time posting of financial documents, ensuring that transactions are reflected immediately in the SAP system. This supports timely decision-making and reporting.

  5. Custom Applications: Developers can leverage this BAPI to build custom applications that interact with SAP FI, tailored to specific business needs.

  6. Batch Processing: While it supports real-time processing, the BAPI can also be used for batch processing of financial documents, allowing for efficient handling of large volumes of data.

IMPLEMENTATION

STEP 1 :-  Go to tcode - se37 (Function Builder)

  • In se37 we will display our Bapi  BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST.

  •  Execute The Source Code.
  • A new window will appear where now we have to enter data to generate the document number. 

 

 

 

STEP 2) :-  ENTER THE MANDATORY FIELDS REQUIRED TO POST THE DOCUMENT NUMBER IN BKPF TABLE.

IMPORTING PARAMETERS

Header Data

All mandatory fields are required in the Document header.

FIELD NAME

User Name

USNAM

Company Code

COMP_CODE

Document Date

DOC_DATE

Posting Date

PSTING_DATE

Document Type

DOC_TYPE

 

TEST DATA:

USNAM

COMP_CODE

DOC_DATE

PSTING_DATE

DOC_TYPE

BASANTJ

1710

13.03.2024

13.03.2024

RV

 

 

 

TABLES:

1)      ACCOUNTGL 

 

Description

Parameter for transferring G/L account line items

The following field must be filled out:

  • GL_ACCOUNT    G/L account

The currency fields are not contained in this parameter. They must be transferred separately in the parameter CurrencyAmount for the various currency types and currencies. The system creates a link to the relevant line item via the line item number ITEMNO_ACC, which for this reason must be unique for each line item.

FIELD NAME

Item account number

ITEMNO_ACC

GL account

GL_ACCOUNT

Item text

ITEM_TEXT

Company code

COMP_CODE

Posting date

PSTNG_DATE

 

TEST DATA

ITEMNO_ACC

GL_ACCOUNT

ITEM_TEXT

COMP_CODE

PSTNG_DATE

0000000001

10010001

LINE ITEM 1

1710

13.03.2024

 

 

 

 

1)      Account receivable

 

Description

Parameter for transferring customer line items

If the CUSTOMER number is not given, only the general ledger is updated. In this case, an entry must be made in the field GL_ACCOUNT (G/L account for general ledger accounting).

The currency fields are not contained in this parameter. They must be transferred separately in the parameter CurrencyAmount for the various currency types and currencies. The system creates a link to the relevant line item via the line item number ITEMNO_ACC, which for this reason must be unique for each line item.

 

FIELD NAME

Item account number

ITEMNO_ACC

Customer number

CUSTOMER

Reference key

REF_KEY_1

Company code

COMP_CODE

Item text

ITEM_TEXT

 

TEST DATA

ITEMNO_ACC

CUSTOMER

REF_KEY_1

COMP_CODE

ITEM_TEXT

0000000002

17100001

XYZ_TEST_2

1710

CUSTOMER ENTRY

 

 

 

 

 

 

2)      Currency Amount

 

Description

This parameter describes the currency fields for a particular line item.
The parameter is a required field for the method POST. If the parameter is not maintained for the method CHECK, only a general account assignment check is carried out. Individual line items or several documents can be checked at once in this way.

The following fields must be filled out:

  • CURRENCY     Currency key
  • AMT_DOCCUR    Currency amount

Value range

The field CURR_TYPE (currency type) describes the role played by a currency in the SAP System.
The currency type can take on the following values, for example:

  • 00      Transaction currency
  • 10      Company code currency
  • 20      Controlling area currency
  • 30      Group currency
  • 40      Hard currency
  • 50      Index-based currency
  • 60      Global company currency

If an entry is not made in the field CURR_TYPE, the default value "00" is retained.

If only one data record is transferred in transaction currency for each line item, the field CURR_TYPE does not need to be filled out. If several data records are transferred for each line item, the currency type must be entered for each record.

FIELD NAME

Items account number

ITEMNO_ACC

Currency

CURRENCY

Amount

AMT_DOCCUR

 

TEST DATA

ITEMNO_ACC

CURRENCY

AMT_DOCCUR

0000000001

USD

2.000,0000-

0000000002

USD

  2.000,0000

 

 

 

Exporting Parameters.

The information from this document must be transferred when the FI/CO interface is called up. The source information is updated in all accounting documents, and is made up of the following fields.

  • AWTYP (OBJ_TYPE) Reference transaction
    Sender ID
  • AWKEY (OBJ_KEY) Reference key
    Source document number

The reference key is made up of:

    • AWREF Reference document number (10 digits)
      Source document number
    • AWORG Reference organizational unit (10 digits)
      Number range ID (if required)
  • AWSYS (OBJ_SYS) Logical system
    Logical system of source document

 

 

Posting Document Number.

  1. First go to se37 and click on function module.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Upload Your test data directory and execute it and press back and again execute it to final commit the changes in BKPF table ( For which we used BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT ).

 

  • Now go to BKPF Table To find the entry.
  • Further Odata can be create using BKPF and BSEG tables.

The Data will be stored in BKPF and BSEG  table.

entry.title: Call transaction FB02 to 3rd screen
entry.content:
I need to make a call transaction in fb02, however on the second screen there are many items and I need to double click on the last one (supplier record):
This number of items may vary, so the number of times I click on the scroll button also 

Does anyone know a way for me to go to the third screen already displaying the supplier item? I tried set parameter but it just skips the first screen and I need to go to the third screen.

entry.title: How to remove the transportation planning type in ewm delivery
entry.content:
I would like to do the goods issue in EWM but not able to do it.

I would like to remove this indicator. Done the configuration but its not getting updated in new deliveries.

Have checked some blogs and there its mentioned that BF is causing this issue. But those BF i am not able to deactivate in SFW5

How to solve this

entry.title: Re: Cannot retrieve session with VBA
entry.content: Did you enable scripting in the SAPGUI?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow from S/4HANA to Azure Data Lake
entry.content:
@Srikanth Yes, it's the same process works for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and Azure Data Lake as a target. You can create a replication flow and use as a source either CDS Views or local tables with primary keys from SAP Datasphere . Of course you need to load data from CDS Views from S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to the local tables in case if you'd like to use the local tables.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Fiori RfQ: Receival of supplier’s proposals
entry.content:
Hi,

I hope this is the right forum:

We are looking into implementing Fiori RfQ.

As far as I can tell the only option to receive proposals is to receive an email from a supplier and manually enter the information in Fiori.

Is there a way to enable the suppliers to enter their information in somekind of (web-based) form and transmit it to Fiori? To then automatically create the proposal in RfQ?

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Flexible Workflow Custom Scenario development with My Inbox Integration
entry.content:
Hi Naveen,

Very Nice blog. Thank you for sharing.

entry.title: Re: Can B1if install on server other than DB server ?
entry.content: Hello Stefan, can you point to the webpage showing that recommendation ? Thanks,

entry.title: How to Hide secret BOM Recipe?
entry.content:
Customer wants to hide the BOM recipe because these are unique and confidential. So actual BOM component and Material code should not be shown to the users, so here requirement is to show a virtual material code and material name?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Hide secret BOM Recipe?
entry.content: There is no functionality to show a virtual material code but the Business Catalog SAP_PLM_BC_MBOM_PC can be used which is needed to access the BOM apps like Maintain Bill of Material app to control who views the actual BOM using the restrictions 'Authorization Group for Product' and 'Authorization Group for BOM Usage'. If the User does not have the correct authorisation group assigned then the BOM is hidden to the unauthorised user

entry.title: Re: [BUG] blog shows up under untagged tag
entry.content: @audreystevenson Ok, thanks for the update.

entry.title: Re: Edit in the MIGO table?
entry.content:
Editing tables directly in SAP, especially critical tables like MIGO (Material Document), is not recommended due to potential data inconsistencies and system errors. Making changes directly to tables can lead to serious consequences such as data corruption, inconsistency in transactions, and even system failures.

Instead, if you need to make changes to material documents or any other SAP data, you should use SAP standard transactions or programs designed for those purposes. For example, to make changes to material documents, you can use transactions like MIGO (Goods Movement), MB1A (Goods Issue), MB1C (Other Goods Receipts), etc., depending on the type of document you need to edit.

If the standard SAP transactions do not meet your specific requirements, consider consulting with your SAP administrator or developer to explore custom solutions that adhere to SAP best practices and ensure data integrity and system stability.

entry.title: pls share the t code for update invoice no and date
entry.content: pls share the t code for update invoice no and date

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
entry.content:
Jocelyn,

Since all our systems are one-client, do you recommend creating Business Catalogs in CONF scope? or should we create them in CUST? 

Is there an advantage between CONF and CUST scopes for Business Catalogs, if there is only one client. We are on S/4HANA 2020 FPS02.

Thanks

entry.title: Gender field (custom) in Quick Apply to Late Stage
entry.content:
Hello- we are currently working on quick apply. One of the issues we are having is when you initially apply as a US candidate, you are required to answer the knockout questions and self-identify EEO/Disability/Veteran questions.

When they move onto late-stage application, the only field that doesn’t auto-populate over with their selection is Gender. What we learned form our support partner is since this field is not a SF standard field ‘Male’ or ‘Female’, and it is custom, it will not populate over. They confirmed they have other customers who have this behavior as well and a KBA confirmed this as well for us.

If you use Quick Apply, what is the behavior on your end when candidate is in late stage (if you are using a custom field)? Is it requiring Gender to be answered? We are hoping to have fewer manual entries for the candidate and wanted to see if there's a workaround without having to change from customer to standard field.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to stop HTML Viewer from refreshing?
entry.content: Are you owning the code where the HTML viewer is used? If so, you shouldn't create a new instance of the HTML control in GUI each roundtrip. Instead the instance of the Cl_GUI_HTML_VIEWER should be a global variable in the program and only be instantiated at the very beginning.

entry.title: SAP Build Code - Create Application Logic with Joule
entry.content:
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-code-test-drive.html

When I Open in Graphical Modeler, I got a blank screen, do you know why :

"In the Storyboard, click on the Purchases entity under Services, and select Open in Graphical Modeler".

entry.title: Re: Restriction of negative balance based on accrual amou...
entry.content: Hi @beyreli , I've written this blog with a possible solution for this requirement. Hope it helps. Thank you, Soraia

entry.title: Re: Call transaction FB02 to 3rd screen
entry.content: Call the transactuion via ABAP4_CALL_TRANSACTION. Wrap this call in a RFC-Call starting new task.
